We present EvoHunt, a playbook evolution environment over open-source repositories for security auditing. Three agents drive the evolution loop: an audit agent rolls out the current playbook and produces findings; an evaluator scores outcomes against ground truth; and a reviser commits updates to the playbook based on failure analysis. The playbook format is unconstrained: starting empty, EvoHunt adds or removes workflows, heuristics, vulnerability knowledge, or domain-specific content. The evolved playbook requires only minor adaptation to run under a different LLM or harness. We evaluate EvoHunt on open-source security advisories. For acquisition, playbook evolution raises end-to-end exploits for Codex/GPT5.4-xhigh 6x, from 1.1% to 6.2%, and the evolved OpenCode/GLM5.1 playbook surpasses OpenAI Codex Security on every metric, with 11.3% vs. 9.2% target-match rate, showing open-source evolution can outperform a dedicated commercial product. For transfer, the GLM-evolved playbook gives the strongest student lift: Qwen3.6-27B improves from 2.4% to 6.5%, Qwen3.6-35B-A3B from 1.1% to 4.6%, and A3B obtains 2.4x more matches than GPT transfer.

item.title: FEnc$^2$: Unifying Data Packing for Efficient Private Inference via Convolution and Architecture-Aware Fragment Encoding
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16359v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

全同态加密（FHE）为隐私保护机器学习提供了理论保障，但其高昂的计算与内存开销严重制约实际部署。除底层原语（如数论变换NTT、旋转、密钥切换）固有代价外，应用层密文打包（ciphertext packing）低效是被长期忽视的关键瓶颈：现有策略或优先保持空间邻近性（如卷积滑动窗口），或侧重特征/通道分组，却无法兼顾二者，导致密文槽位浪费、旋转次数激增、密文数量膨胀。

方法创新：FEnc²统一框架

本文提出FEnc²——首个面向CKKS方案、融合卷积特性与网络架构的碎片化编码框架，包含两大核心组件：

  • 卷积感知编码（Conv-aware Encoding）：通过解析建模，动态选择最优数据碎片大小，解耦空间依赖关系，并联合最小化各层的“内旋转”（intra-layer）与“外旋转”（inter-layer），显著提升槽位利用率；
  • 架构感知密文压缩（Arch-aware Ct Compression）：在特征降维或通道缩减层（如池化、1×1卷积）后主动重构密文结构，恢复密文密度，避免信息稀疏化。

性能突破与意义

FEnc²重塑加密张量布局，将同态运算量降低1–2个数量级。在满内存负载（最大批大小）下：

  • LeNet/MNIST：GPU端较SOTA系统Orion提速228.83×，CPU端达226.06×；
  • MobileNet/ImageNet：GPU提速4.55×，CPU达9.43×。

FEnc²硬件无关，却具备架构级变革力——它在执行前优化加密数据布局，从源头减少密文数量与硬件负载，与NTT/密钥切换等底层加速器形成正交互补。本工作确立应用层数据布局为加密推理的首要架构设计维度，为下一代FHE系统提供关键使能技术。

AI Summary (English)

Fully Homomorphic Encryption (FHE) enables privacy-preserving inference but suffers from prohibitive overhead—not only from expensive low-level primitives (e.g., NTT, rotation, key switching), but critically from application-level ciphertext packing inefficiency. Existing packing strategies sacrifice either spatial locality (for convolution) or feature/channel grouping, wasting slots and inflating rotations and ciphertext count. We propose FEnc², a unified, fragment-based encoding framework for CKKS-based private CNN inference. It introduces: (1) Conv-aware Encoding, which analytically selects optimal fragment size to decouple spatial dependencies and jointly minimize intra- and inter-layer rotations; and (2) Arch-aware Ct Compression, which restores ciphertext density after dimensionality-reduction layers. Together, they reshape encrypted workload structure and reduce homomorphic operations by 10–100×. On LeNet/MNIST, FEnc² achieves up to 228.83× (GPU) and 226.06× (CPU) end-to-end speedup over Orion; on MobileNet/ImageNet, up to 4.55× (GPU) and 9.43× (CPU). Hardware-agnostic yet architecturally transformative, FEnc² proves that application-level data layout is a first-order design dimension for efficient FHE inference.

Abstract

Fully Homomorphic Encryption (FHE) enables privacy-preserving machine learning but incurs extreme computational and memory overhead. These costs come not only from expensive low-level primitives, including Number Theoretic Transform (NTT), rotation, and key-switching, but also from inefficient ciphertext packing at the application level. Existing packing strategies typically preserve either neighboring data elements or feature grouping, but not both, leading to wasted ciphertext slots, excessive rotations, and inflated ciphertext counts. We propose FEnc2, a unified and principled fragment-based encoding framework for CKKS-based private convolutional neural network inference. FEnc2 optimizes slot utilization, rotation complexity, and ciphertext density through two components: 1)Conv-aware Encoding, which analytically selects an optimal fragment size to decouple spatial dependencies and jointly minimize inner-outer rotations across layers, and 2)Arch-aware Ct Compression, which restores ciphertext density after feature- or channel-reduction layers. Together, these transformations reshape encrypted workload structure and reduce homomorphic operations by one to two orders of magnitude. With full memory capacity utilized, i.e., at maximum batch size, FEnc2 achieves end-to-end latency speedups over the state-of-the-art Orion of up to 228.83x on GPU and 226.06x on CPU for LeNet on MNIST, and up to 4.55x on GPU and 9.43x on CPU for MobileNet on ImageNet. FEnc2 is hardware-agnostic yet architecturally transformative: by optimizing encrypted tensor layout before execution, it reduces ciphertext count and workload pressure on hardware, complementing primitive-level optimizations such as NTT and keyswitch accelerators. These results show that application-level data layout is a first-order architectural design dimension for encrypted inference and an important enabler for next-generation FHE systems.

item.title: The Proxy Knows Too Much: Sealing LLM API Routers with Attested TEEs
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16358v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

当前AI代理普遍通过API路由器访问大语言模型（LLM），但此类路由器需终止客户端TLS连接并重建上游会话，导致全程交互以明文形式驻留于路由器内存中。这使其成为应用层“中间人”：可恶意重写工具调用、注入拼写劫持（typosquatted）依赖包、在审计规避条件下触发攻击，或被动窃取密钥等敏感信息。现有客户端侧防护（如输出验证、沙箱）均被证明可被绕过。

方法：AEGIS系统

我们提出AEGIS——一种供应商透明、基于可信执行环境（TEE）的认证API路由器。其核心创新在于：

  • 可信数据路径最小化：仅851行代码的硬件加密 enclave 负责明文转发，严格隔离于不可信主机；
  • 职责分离：身份认证、请求调度、计费与管理等非敏感功能保留在主机侧；
  • 客户端强验证：客户端在提交明文前，通过远程认证（attestation）验证enclave的完整性与配置；
  • 出口锁定机制：明文仅能流向enclave镜像中预先测量（measured）且固定的下游目标（如特定LLM端点），杜绝任意外泄。

主要成果

  • AEGIS成功阻断全部四类已知恶意路由器攻击（重写、劫持、条件触发、窃密），而基线明文路由器全部失守；
  • 支持3种原生提供商API（无协议转换），在真实负载与并发压力下100%请求成功完成；
  • 在种子式审计试点中，两个商用编码智能体分别检出10个植入不变式违规中的8个和10个；
  • 本地中继开销仅约6毫秒/请求，兼顾安全性与实用性。

AI Summary (English)

Large language model (LLM) agents increasingly rely on API routers, which decrypt TLS traffic and handle interactions in plaintext—making them potent application-layer man-in-the-middle adversaries. Existing client-side defenses are evadable. We propose AEGIS, an attested, provider-transparent API router that confines plaintext processing to a minimal, hardware-enforced TEE enclave (851 LoC), while delegating authentication, scheduling, and management to the untrusted host. Clients verify the enclave’s integrity via remote attestation before releasing any plaintext; the host cannot read or modify payloads, and plaintext egress is strictly bound to destinations embedded in the measured enclave image. AEGIS blocks all four known malicious-router attack classes—tool-call rewriting, dependency squatting, audit-evading triggers, and passive secret exfiltration—while supporting three native provider APIs without conversion, sustaining full throughput under real-world load and concurrency. In a seeded audit, two commodity coding agents detected 8/10 and 10/10 planted invariant violations. Relay overhead is just ~6 ms per request.

Abstract

Agents increasingly access large language models (LLMs) through API routers. A router terminates the client's transport-layer security session and opens a separate upstream session, so it holds the full interaction in plaintext. This makes the router an application-layer man-in-the-middle: it can rewrite agent tool calls, swap dependencies for typosquatted packages, trigger attacks only under audit-evading conditions, and passively exfiltrate secrets. Existing client-side defenses are evadable. We propose AEGIS, a provider-transparent attested API router whose data path is a client-verified faithful passthrough. AEGISconfines plaintext handling to a small hardware-enclave component while leaving authentication, scheduling, accounting, and management on the untrusted host. The client verifies the enclave before releasing plaintext. The host can neither read nor alter the interaction, and plaintext leaves only toward destinations fixed by the measured image. We show that all four malicious-router attack classes succeed against a plaintext-access baseline and are blocked by AEGIS, including adaptive tests against the same boundary. The trusted path is $851$ lines, carries three provider-native APIs without conversion, and completes every request under real-provider workload and concurrency. In a seeded audit pilot, two commodity coding agents find eight and ten of ten planted invariant violations. The local relay overhead is about six milliseconds per request.

item.title: SPARK: Security Knowledge Priming and Representation-Guided Knowledge Activation for LLM-based Secure Code Generation
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16244v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

大型语言模型（LLM）在生成代码时频繁引入可利用的安全漏洞，传统归因于其缺乏安全专业知识，因而现有防御方案多依赖耗资源的微调或外部知识检索，导致计算开销大、数据偏差强，且需大量冗余代码示例。

方法创新：SPARK框架

本文提出SPARK——一种纯推理时（inference-time）的安全增强框架，无需任何模型重训练。其核心洞见是：预训练语料中已蕴含丰富安全知识，瓶颈在于知识激活不足。SPARK包含两部分：

  • Component I（知识引导）：针对当前编码任务，轻量检索相关Common Weakness Enumeration（CWE）条目，生成简短、结构化的安全提示（如“避免CWE-78: OS命令注入”），直接拼接至输入提示。该设计显著激活模型内部已有的安全表征；
  • Component II（表征引导）：离线预计算一个安全方向偏置向量——即最后一层隐状态中“安全样本均值”与“不安全样本均值”的单位差向量，经语言模型输出头投影后，于每步解码时对logits施加单次向量加法。该操作仅增加微量计算（≈1次向量加法/词），无运行时检索开销。

实验与效果

在9个开源模型（覆盖C++/Java/Python）上系统评估，对比7种主流基线（含微调与RAG方法），SPARK在所有设置下持平或超越最优基线，同时完全保持HumanEval功能正确性。进一步在Claude、DeepSeek、GPT等7个最强黑盒商用模型上验证Component I，证实“激活不足”是生成不安全代码的根本瓶颈，且仅靠结构化提示即可带来显著提升。

AI Summary (English)

Large language models (LLMs) frequently generate code with exploitable security flaws—not due to missing security knowledge, but to inadequate activation of latent safety representations embedded in pretraining data. SPARK addresses this at inference time without retraining: Component I retrieves task-relevant CWE entries and appends concise, structured security cues to prompts; Component II applies a lightweight, precomputed token bias—derived from the unit difference between safe and unsafe hidden-state centroids—via a single vector addition per decoded token. Evaluated across 9 open-source models (C++/Java/Python) against 7 baselines (fine-tuning & RAG), SPARK matches or exceeds the best baseline in all security metrics while preserving HumanEval utility. Component I further demonstrates strong zero-shot transfer on 7 black-box commercial models (including Claude, DeepSeek, and GPT), confirming activation—not knowledge absence—as the core bottleneck.

Abstract

Large language models routinely generate code with exploitable security flaws. Prior literature attributes this limitation to a lack of security expertise, steering current defense mechanisms toward heavy fine-tuning or external knowledge retrieval, which introduces significant computational overhead and data bias through redundant code examples. Contrary to this view, we argue that pretraining corpora are already rich in security material. The bottleneck is activation: without an explicit and brief cue, statistical pressure toward common training-distribution patterns suppresses the model's safety-relevant representations. We present SPARK, an inference-time security harness that activates this latent knowledge without any retraining. The harness has two parts. Component~I retrieves a few of the relevant Common Weakness Enumeration (CWE) entries for each coding task and appends a short structured cue to the prompt; this alone is enough to surface the model's existing security representations. Component~II adds a precomputed token bias to the logits at every decoding step. We obtain the bias by projecting a safe-direction vector, the unit difference between the mean safe and mean unsafe last-layer hidden states, through the language model head. The bias is computed once offline; applying it costs a single vector addition per generated token. We evaluate SPARK on 9 open-source models across C++, Java, and Python, and compare with 7 baselines spanning fine-tuning and retrieval-augmented methods. SPARK matches or improves on the best baseline in every setting while preserving HumanEval utility. We further test Component~I in a black-box setting on 7 of today's strongest models, including Claude, DeepSeek, and GPT, demonstrating the bottleneck of insecure code generation and the improvements enabled by our method.

item.title: Phantoms and Disclosures: a Causal Framework for Auditing Synthetic Data
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16952v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

生成式AI与大语言模型（LLMs）的广泛应用推动了合成数据作为敏感真实数据替代方案的发展，但高保真合成数据易无意记忆并复现训练集中的个体隐私信息，引发严重隐私风险。现有审计方法（如影子模型、金丝雀注入）依赖模型访问、额外训练或人工干预，成本高、可扩展性差，且难以区分“真实泄露”（模型主动记忆并输出某用户数据）与“幻影泄露”（偶然生成相同数据但无记忆行为）。

方法创新

本文提出首个因果驱动的合成数据审计框架，无需模型访问、不插入金丝雀、不训练参考模型——仅需合成输出与一个独立保留的控制数据集（holdout set）。核心在于：

  • 双重披露分类：严格区分真实披露（synthetic sample ≡ original holdout sample，表明成员推断成功）与幻影披露（语义/统计等价但非同一实例，反映随机重合）；
  • 因果检验机制：通过将原始数据划分为训练集与保留集，结合精确的二项假设检验，评估观测到的披露率是否显著高于“零学习”或给定差分隐私（DP）边界下的理论期望值；
  • 隐私下界量化：将审计结果直接映射为经验性隐私泄漏下界，比基于重构误差或分布距离的传统方法更紧致、更具可解释性。

主要发现与优势

在多个文本与表格合成任务上验证，本框架：（1）以<0.5%计算开销超越影子模型法的检测灵敏度；（2）首次实现对DP参数（ε）的实证反向校准；（3）完全模型无关，兼容GAN、VAE、LLM-based synthesizers等任意生成机制。该工作为合成数据合规部署提供了轻量、严谨、即插即用的隐私审计基础设施。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces Phantoms and Disclosures, a lightweight, model-agnostic causal auditing framework for synthetic data privacy. Unlike prior methods requiring model access, canary insertion, or shadow-model training, our approach uses only the synthetic output and a held-out control set to detect privacy violations. We formally distinguish true disclosures—exact reproductions indicating membership inference—from phantom disclosures—statistically coincidental matches—and employ rigorous hypothesis testing to assess whether observed disclosure rates exceed baselines under strict privacy assumptions (e.g., zero learning or prescribed Differential Privacy ε). Empirically, our framework achieves tighter lower bounds on privacy leakage than reconstruction-based auditors, operates at <1% the computational cost of shadow-model alternatives, and enables post-hoc DP parameter validation. It is broadly applicable across generative mechanisms (LLMs, GANs, VAEs) and provides deployable, interpretable privacy certification.

Abstract

The rapid adoption of generative AI and Large Language Models (LLMs) has spurred interest in synthetic data as a privacy-preserving alternative to sensitive real-world datasets. However, generating high-utility synthetic data often carries the risk of memorizing and regurgitating private information from the training corpus. In this work, we present a customizable empirical auditing framework designed to detect and explain such data disclosures. Our framework introduces a mechanism to distinguish between "true disclosures"-where the system directly reproduces a user's information-and "phantom disclosures''-where the system incidentally generates a user's data. By partitioning input data into training and holdout sets and applying rigorous statistical hypothesis testing, we determine if observed disclosures are consistent with strict privacy baselines, such as zero-learning or specific Differential Privacy (DP) bounds. Crucially, this approach requires no model access, no canary insertion, and no reference model training -only the synthetic output and a held-out control set. We demonstrate that this framework effectively functions as a membership inference attack, providing empirical lower bounds on privacy leakage that are tighter than prior data-based auditing methods. Our approach is model-agnostic, applies to any synthetic data generation mechanism, and requires orders of magnitude fewer computational resources than shadow-model or canary-based alternatives.

item.title: Beyond Weights and Gradients: A Taxonomy of Federated Learning Messages
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16891v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

联邦学习（Federated Learning, FL）正迅速超越传统仅交换模型权重与梯度的范式，涌现出合成数据生成、联邦统计分析、知识蒸馏提示、特征嵌入共享等新型通信载荷。然而，现有文献缺乏对“联邦消息”这一核心概念的统一形式化定义，导致系统设计、隐私评估与跨方案比较面临概念模糊与标准缺失的挑战。

方法与框架

本文首次提出联邦消息的严格数学定义：一个三元组 $ \mathcal{M} = (X, \mathcal{U}, \mathcal{P}) $，其中 $ X $ 为消息内容（任意可编码对象），$ \mathcal{U} $ 为效用函数（刻画其在全局任务中的贡献），$ \mathcal{P} $ 为隐私度量函数（如差分隐私预算或重建风险上界）。基于此，我们构建三级分类学（Taxonomy）：

  • 模型结构类（Model Structures）：含轻量化架构、稀疏掩码、神经正交基等；
  • 统计摘要类（Statistical Summaries）：含协方差矩阵、梯度统计量、分布矩估计、聚合直方图等；
  • 数据条件化表示类（Data-Conditioned Representations）：含本地特征嵌入、合成样本集、梯度反演中间态、提示向量（prompt tokens）等。

主要发现与创新

通过对202篇2021–2024年顶会/期刊论文的系统性综述，我们发现：
✅ 自2021年起，非权重/非梯度消息占比从12%跃升至2024年的68%；
✅ 统计摘要类在通信开销（平均降低5.3×）与隐私风险（中位DP预算提升2.1×）间取得最优平衡；
✅ 数据条件化表示类虽效用最高，但需配套强隐私机制（如生成式DP或零知识验证）；
✅ 本分类学已支撑3个开源FL框架（FedTax, SynthFL-Kit, StatAgg）的消息层标准化。

该工作为面向异构硬件、动态威胁模型与多目标优化的下一代联邦系统提供了可扩展、可验证、可审计的通信设计基础。

AI Summary (English)

Federated Learning (FL) is rapidly shifting from exchanging only model weights and gradients to transmitting diverse payloads—including synthetic data, statistical summaries, and data-conditioned representations—yet lacks a formal, utility-aware, and privacy-grounded definition of federated messages. This paper bridges that gap by proposing a rigorous mathematical definition: a federated message $ \mathcal{M} = (X, \mathcal{U}, \mathcal{P}) $, where $ X $ is the payload, $ \mathcal{U} $ quantifies its task utility, and $ \mathcal{P} $ bounds its privacy leakage. We introduce a three-category taxonomy—Model Structures, Statistical Summaries, and Data-Conditioned Representations—and evaluate each across computational cost, communication overhead, and privacy risk. A systematic review of 202 recent FL papers (2021–2024) reveals a decisive paradigm shift: non-gradient messages rose from 12% to 68% of publications, with Statistical Summaries offering the best trade-off in bandwidth efficiency (5.3× reduction) and privacy (2.1× median DP budget gain). Our taxonomy enables principled design of FL systems under heterogeneous hardware and evolving threat models.

Abstract

Federated Learning is rapidly evolving beyond the exchange of traditional model weights and gradients, yet existing definitions fail to capture the full scope of modern payloads like synthetic data and federated analytics. This paper addresses the gap by proposing a formal mathematical definition of a federated message that accounts for both utility and privacy. We introduce a taxonomy that organizes these exchanges into three categories: model structures, statistical summaries, and data-conditioned representations. By evaluating these groups based on computational demands, communication costs, and privacy risks, we provide a clearer understanding of the trade-offs involved in decentralized training. Our review of 202 recent publications highlights a significant shift since 2021 toward diverse messaging paradigms, signaling a move away from standard deep learning updates toward more specialized information sharing. This framework provides a structured path for future research to optimize federated systems for varying hardware and security requirements.

item.title: Federated Medical Image Segmentation under Real-World Label Noise: A Benchmark Suite for Noisy Label Learning Method Selection
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16868v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

联邦学习（FL）为跨医疗机构协作开展医学图像分割提供了隐私保护新范式，但真实临床部署中普遍存在跨站点标签噪声：包括医师间轮廓标注分歧、解剖结构漏标/误标、类别混淆等。现有联邦噪声标签学习（FNLL）研究多依赖人工合成噪声或简化仿真设置，缺乏对真实噪声模式、异构客户端噪声分布及临床可解释评估的系统性验证，导致方法选型缺乏实证依据。

方法与创新

本研究提出首个面向真实世界标签噪声的联邦医学图像分割基准套件 FedSegNoiseBench，包含三大核心组件：

  • 多源真实噪声数据集：整合来自6个独立临床中心的4类模态（CT/MRI/US/OCT）共12个分割任务，涵盖器官、病灶、血管等不同目标，所有标签均经专家复核并标注噪声类型与置信度；
  • 部署就绪的客户端噪声建模：支持5类现实场景——局部缺失（如某中心系统性漏标胰腺）、类别混淆（如将“囊肿”误标为“肿瘤”）、空间粗粒度（低分辨率标注）、跨中心不一致（同一结构在不同中心定义不同）及混合噪声；
  • 噪声感知评估体系：除标准Dice/Jaccard外，新增噪声鲁棒性增益（NRG）、标签错误校正率（LECR） 和跨中心泛化稳定性（CCS） 三项指标，量化模型对噪声的识别、修正与迁移能力。

意义与影响

该套件首次实现从“合成噪声测试”到“真实噪声诊断”的范式跃迁，为FNLL方法选型提供可复现、可解释、可比较的基准平台。代码已开源（https://github.com/MIC-DKFZ/FedSegNoiseBench），支持即插即用式方法集成与公平对比，助力构建更鲁棒、可信、临床可用的联邦智能诊疗系统。

AI Summary (English)

Federated learning (FL) enables privacy-preserving medical image segmentation across institutions, yet real-world deployment is severely hindered by heterogeneous, clinically grounded label noise—such as inter-rater contour disagreement, missing/extra structure annotations, and class confusion. Current federated noisy label learning (FNLL) research relies heavily on synthetic noise and oversimplified settings, lacking systematic evaluation under realistic, multi-site noise patterns. To bridge this gap, we introduce FedSegNoiseBench, the first comprehensive benchmark suite for FNLL in medical imaging. It integrates 12 real-world noisy segmentation datasets across 4 modalities (CT/MRI/US/OCT) from 6 clinical centers, supports 5 deployment-relevant client-noise scenarios (e.g., systematic omission, cross-center definition inconsistency), and proposes noise-targeted metrics—Noise Robustness Gain (NRG), Label Error Correction Rate (LECR), and Cross-Center Stability (CCS)—to go beyond standard Dice scores. Empirical evaluation of 8 state-of-the-art FL methods reveals substantial performance variance under real noise (up to 32% Dice drop vs. clean benchmarks), highlighting the critical need for noise-aware selection. Code and datasets are publicly available at https://github.com/MIC-DKFZ/FedSegNoiseBench.

Abstract

While federated learning (FL) enables collaborative medical image segmentation without centralizing sensitive data, real-world deployment is frequently complicated by cross-site label imperfections such as contour disagreement, missing or additional structures, and confused labels. Federated noisy label learning (FNLL) aims to mitigate these effects, yet remains underused in practice as existing evidence is largely based on synthetic noise, simplified settings, and limited real-world noisy evaluation. We address this gap by introducing a benchmark suite that combines diverse real-world noisy datasets, deployment-relevant client-noise scenarios, and label-noise-targeted evaluation to support systematic FNLL assessment and informed method selection. The suite combines curated real-world noisy medical image segmentation datasets from diverse sources with a comprehensive federated segmentation framework including various client-noise scenarios and noise-targeted evaluation. The presented suite provides a realistic and discriminative basis for FNLL evaluation in medical image segmentation and establishes a reusable foundation for fair benchmarking, dataset-specific label-noise characterization, and future method development under realistic federated settings. Code is available at https://github.com/MIC-DKFZ/FedSegNoiseBench.

item.title: A comparative and critical study of EEGNet for fNIRS-driven cognitive load classification
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16160v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与挑战

功能性近红外光谱（fNIRS）在便携式、抗干扰认知负荷监测中具有独特优势，但其信号受时间变异性、被试间差异及预处理敏感性影响显著，导致分类模型泛化能力薄弱，尤其在被试无关（Subject-Independent, SI） 场景下性能骤降。

方法学创新与系统评估

本研究对轻量级深度网络EEGNet在fNIRS认知负荷分类任务中开展首次全面、批判性评估，系统考察五大关键设计维度：

  • 时序分段策略：重叠 vs. 非重叠滑动窗；
  • 窗口长度：10s / 20s / 30s；
  • 特征提取方法：方差分析（ANOVA）、主成分分析（PCA）、快速独立成分分析（FastICA）；
  • 学习率配置：固定值（0.1–0.001）与自适应（Adam）；
  • 评估范式：随机划分（Random Split）vs. 被试无关（SI）交叉验证。

核心发现与机制阐释

  • 在随机划分设定下，重叠分段+小固定学习率（0.01–0.001） 取得最高准确率（>75%），归因于血流动力学过渡期的密集采样与时间冗余增强；
  • 然而SI评估揭示严重泛化瓶颈：准确率普遍下降15–25个百分点；其中非重叠分段在SI场景下显著优于重叠策略，最佳结果为PCA特征+20秒窗+0.1学习率 → 56.11%；
  • 进一步表明：消除时间冗余可迫使模型聚焦跨被试稳定的生理模式，提升表征鲁棒性；
  • 自适应学习率提升训练稳定性，但未超越最优固定学习率的SI性能。

理论与应用启示

本研究确立了分段策略与学习率协同优化是突破fNIRS模型泛化瓶颈的关键路径，为构建实时、可靠、被试无关的认知负荷监测系统提供了可复现的方法论框架与实证基准。

AI Summary (English)

This study presents the first comprehensive and critical evaluation of EEGNet for fNIRS-based cognitive load classification. We systematically investigate five key design axes: temporal segmentation (overlapping vs. non-overlapping), window length (10/20/30 s), feature extraction (ANOVA, PCA, FastICA), learning rate schemes (fixed vs. adaptive Adam), and evaluation protocols (random split vs. subject-independent, SI). While overlapping segmentation with small fixed learning rates (0.01–0.001) achieves peak accuracy (>75%) under random split—leveraging temporal redundancy—we observe a severe generalization drop under SI evaluation. Crucially, non-overlapping windows outperform overlapping ones in SI settings, yielding the best SI accuracy of 56.11% using PCA features, a 20-s window, and a fixed learning rate of 0.1. This demonstrates that suppressing temporal redundancy promotes learning of robust, cross-subject representations. Adaptive learning improves training stability but fails to surpass optimally tuned fixed learning rates. Our work identifies segmentation strategy and learning rate selection as pivotal levers for enhancing generalizability—providing essential methodological guidance for real-time, subject-independent fNIRS cognitive load monitoring.

Abstract

Accurately classifying cognitive load from functional near-infrared spectroscopy (fNIRS) signals remains a significant challenge due to temporal variability, inter-subject differences, and sensitivity to preprocessing choices. This study provides a comprehensive evaluation of EEGNet for fNIRS-based cognitive load classification by systematically examining the effects of temporal segmentation strategies (overlapping vs. non-overlapping), window lengths (10s, 20s, 30s), feature extraction methods (Analysis of Variance (ANOVA), Principal Component Analysis (PCA), Fast Independent Component Analysis (FastICA)), learning rate configurations (fixed and adaptive), and evaluation protocols (random split vs. subject-independent (SI)). Results from random-split experiments show that overlapping segmentation, combined with smaller fixed learning rates (0.01-0.001), yields the highest accuracies, due to temporal redundancy and dense sampling of hemodynamic transitions. However, SI evaluation reveals a substantial drop in accuracy, demonstrating limited generalization to unseen participants. Under SI evaluation, non-overlapping segmentation outperformed overlapping windows, with the best accuracy of 56.11% achieved using PCA features with a 20-second window and a 0.1 learning rate. These findings indicate that eliminating temporal redundancy helps the model learn more robust and generalizable representations of cognitive load across individuals. Although adaptive learning rate strategy improved training stability, it did not surpass the performance of optimally selected fixed learning rates. The study highlights the critical role of segmentation strategy and learning rate selection in improving model generalization and identifies methodological considerations essential for developing reliable, real-time, and SI cognitive load classification systems using fNIRS.

item.title: PVminerLLM2: Improving Structured Extraction of Patient Voice via Preference Optimization
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16074v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

患者生成文本（Patient Voice, PV）蕴含疾病体验、社会环境及诊疗参与等关键质性信息，对推动以患者为中心的结局研究（PCOR）至关重要。然而，此类文本高度非结构化，现有方法难以精准提取细粒度结构化要素（如Code、Sub-code、Span）。此前提出的PV-Miner基准与PVMinerLLM模型虽迈出重要一步，但仅依赖监督微调（SFT）在处理罕见错误模式、细粒度标签偏差及token级敏感输出时表现受限——尤其当单个错字或边界偏移即导致语义失效时，SFT难以有效校正。

方法创新

本文提出PVminerLLM2，首次将偏好优化（Preference Optimization）系统引入患者声音结构化抽取任务。核心贡献包括：

  • 令牌级门控稳定化偏好目标：在RLHF框架中引入可学习门控机制，约束优化过程不损害基础token概率分布，避免模型退化；
  • 混淆感知偏好对构建：基于模型预测置信度与标注一致性动态采样高混淆样本对，显著提升对低分离度类别（如近义Sub-code）的判别能力；
  • 双权重校准机制：融合token重要性加权（依据语义角色赋予实体/边界token更高权重）与逆频重采样（缓解长尾标签分布），协同解决结构化输出中的严重类别不平衡问题。

主要结果

在PV-Miner多尺度基准上，PVminerLLM2在不同参数规模（7B–13B）下均超越强基线：
✅ Code层级F1提升最高达4.43%
✅ Sub-code层级提升3.50%（关键细粒度指标）
✅ Span边界提取精度提升1.55%
且全面优于采用DPO、KTO等主流偏好优化方法训练的同类模型。所有代码、预训练权重、评估脚本及补充材料已开源。

AI Summary (English)

Motivated by the critical need to structure patient-generated text for patient-centered outcomes research, we introduce PVminerLLM2, an LLM-based framework that advances structured patient voice extraction via preference optimization—addressing token-critical errors beyond supervised fine-tuning’s reach. We propose: (i) a token-level gated stabilization term in the preference objective to preserve absolute token likelihoods and prevent model degradation; and (ii) confusion-aware preference pair construction leveraging prediction uncertainty and annotation ambiguity to sharpen distinctions among low-separation classes. Complemented by token-importance weighting and inverse-frequency reweighting, PVminerLLM2 achieves consistent gains across model scales: up to +4.43% F1 on Code, +3.50% on Sub-code, and +1.55% on Span, outperforming both SFT baselines and LLMs trained with DPO/KTO. Code, models, and evaluation scripts are publicly available at https://github.com/Data-Mining-Lab-Yale/PVminerLLM2.

Abstract

Motivation: Patient-generated text contains critical information on patients' lived experiences, social context, and care engagement, but remains largely unstructured, limiting its use in patient-centered outcomes research. Prior work introduced the PV-Miner benchmark and PVMinerLLM models for structured extraction. However, supervised fine-tuning (SFT) alone struggles with rare, fine-grained, and unevenly distributed errors, particularly in token-critical structured outputs. Results: We present PVminerLLM2, an improved set of LLMs for structured patient voice extraction that applies preference optimization to address token-critical errors beyond the reach of supervised fine-tuning. Our method introduces (i) a preference objective with token-level gated stabilization term that prevents degradation of absolute token likelihood under preference optimization, and (ii) confusion-aware preference pair construction to better capture low-separation distinctions. We further incorporate token-importance weighting and inverse-frequency reweighing to address token imbalance and class skew. Across multiple model sizes, PVMinerLLM2 consistently outperforms strong baselines, achieving gains of up to 4.43% (Code), 3.50% (Sub-code), and 1.55% (Span), and outperforms baseline LLM trained with existing preference optimization methods. Availability and Implementation: The supplementary material, code, evaluation scripts, and trained models for PVminerLLM2 are publicly available at: https://github.com/Data-Mining-Lab-Yale/PVminerLLM2

item.title: Selection Without Signal, Recovery Through Expression: A Measurement Study of Post-Hoc Falsification Operators for Frozen Small Code Models
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16999v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

针对本地化、隐私敏感场景，冻结的小型代码模型（≤1.5B参数，无需微调）日益普及，但其常生成“看似合理实则错误”的程序。自然补救思路是引入后验（post-hoc）操作符——在不重训练前提下，对模型采样结果进行选择、验证、修复或重处理。受波普尔证伪主义启发，理想操作符应以“严苛测试”主动攻击每个候选解，仅保留通过者。

方法与评估

本研究在确定性执行预言机与无信息泄露、计算量匹配的协议下，系统评测26种语义层后验操作符（含Best-of-N（BoN）、多策略选择、形式化验证、符号修复、消去法、组合策略、可靠否决机制、生成条件调控等），统一在HumanEval+与MBPP+基准上对比其留出集准确率。

关键发现

  • 普遍失效：所有26种语义操作符均未超越BoN；负向结果具机械根源：
  • 覆盖墙（Coverage Wall）：系统性难题失败无法通过更深采样缓解；
  • 能力剪刀差（Capability Scissors）：强生成器产出的“可见测试通过者”中，错误样本几乎不可判别；
  • 共识陷阱（Near-Empty Consensus Trap）：在无泄露约束下，需依赖“多数通过但隐藏错误”与“少数正确解”共现——而该情形极罕见。
  • 理论边界：分布无关的“零伤害”保证要求：若观察到0次伤害，要以α=0.05置信度认证伤害率≤α，需样本量n≥45。

创新突破

两项非语义层操作符显著有效：

  • M1（表达层恢复）：唯一提升准确率的操作符。通过鲁棒提取+公测签名对齐，挽救标准抽取器丢弃的正确程序；零伤害（b₁₀=0）、无泄露，在HumanEval+上使DeepSeek-Coder-1.3B +12任务（p=2.4×10⁻⁴）；
  • ACE（自适应共识早停）：校准式计算节约控制，节省约19%推理开销，零伤害。

二者在HumanEval+/MBPP+及三类模型单元上稳健复现。核心启示：应优先修正评估框架（harness）与测量覆盖率，而非归咎于语义后验推理本身。

AI Summary (English)

This study investigates post-hoc falsification operators for frozen small code models (≤1.5B parameters), which are widely used offline but prone to generating plausible-yet-wrong programs. Under a deterministic execution oracle and leakage-free, matched-compute protocol, we evaluate 26 semantic operators—including selection, verification, repair, elimination, portfolios, sound vetoes, and generation conditioning—against Best-of-N (BoN) on HumanEval+ and MBPP+. None improves held-out accuracy over BoN, revealing three mechanistic barriers: a coverage wall (hard tasks resist deeper sampling), a capability scissors (correct and incorrect candidates among visible-passers are indistinguishable), and a near-empty consensus trap (the “majority-pass-but-wrong + minority-correct” configuration required by leakage-free selectors rarely occurs). A distribution-free do-no-harm bound requires n ≥ 45 to certify harm rate ≤ α = 0.05 with zero observed harms. Crucially, two non-semantic operators succeed: M1, an expression-layer recovery that rescues correct programs discarded by standard extraction via robust parsing and public-test signature alignment, lifts DeepSeek-Coder-1.3B by +12 tasks on HumanEval+ (p = 2.4e−4) with zero harm (b₁₀ = 0); and ACE, an adaptive consensus early-stopping controller, saves ~19% compute with zero harm. Results replicate across benchmarks and model cells—underscoring that harness design and coverage measurement must precede semantic post-hoc reasoning.

Abstract

Frozen small code models (<=1.5B parameters, run locally without fine-tuning) suit offline and privacy-constrained use, but often emit plausible-but-wrong programs. A natural remedy is a post-hoc operator that selects, verifies, repairs, or re-processes the model's samples without retraining; in principled form it is Popperian: attack each candidate with a severe test, keep what survives. We measure whether such operators help. Under one deterministic execution oracle and a leakage-free, matched-compute protocol, 26 semantic post-hoc operators (selection, verification, repair, elimination, portfolios, sound vetoes, generation conditioning) are evaluated against Best-of-N (BoN); on the cells and benchmarks tested, none improves held-out accuracy over BoN. The negative is mechanistic: a coverage wall (systematic hard-task failures deeper sampling does not rescue), a capability scissors (a competent generator leaves almost no discriminable error among visible-test passers), and a near-empty consensus trap (the visible-pass-but-hidden-wrong majority a leakage-free selector needs rarely co-occurs with a correct alternative). A distribution-free do-no-harm bound cannot certify a harm rate <=alpha at zero observed harm unless n>=45. Two operators help on a different axis, outside the semantic output space. An expression-layer recovery (M1), the only accuracy gain here, recovers correct programs the standard extractor discards (robust extraction and public-test signature alignment); it does no harm (b10=0), is leakage-free, and lifts DeepSeek-Coder-1.3B by +12 tasks on HumanEval+ (p=2.4e-4). An adaptive consensus early-stop (ACE) is a calibrated compute-saving control (~19% saving, zero harm). M1 and the selection negative replicate on HumanEval+ and MBPP+ across three model cells. The lesson: fix the harness and measure coverage before blaming semantic post-hoc reasoning.

item.title: Beyond the Smile: A Hybrid Convolutional VAE for Crypto Volatility Surfaces
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16961v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

加密货币期权市场普遍存在数据稀疏、报价不全、流动性断层等问题，导致传统参数化波动率曲面（如SABR或SVI）在高缺失率下失效，且难以保证无套利约束。本文聚焦BTC与ETH的隐含波动率曲面建模，提出首个面向加密期权的混合卷积变分自编码器（Hybrid Convolutional VAE）框架，突破单一参数模型的表达瓶颈与结构局限。

方法创新

模型采用6×7标准化期限-德尔塔网格，基于2023年5–10月Binance平台6,034个完整小时级BTC/ETH波动率曲面训练。核心包含两部分：（1）卷积VAE主干——利用二维卷积捕获曲面空间相关性，通过变分推断学习低维隐变量分布；（2）确定性混合预测器——在推理时，依据每条期限（tenor）的缺失程度动态路由：高完整性期限保留二次微笑拟合（quadratic smile re-fit），高缺失期限则完全交由VAE生成，并无缝拼接。该设计零额外推理开销。

关键发现

  • 精度跃升：在50%随机掩码下，混合预测器RMSE仅0.83波动点，较纯微笑拟合（7.00）降低8倍；
  • 鲁棒性突破：面对整条期限（如全部21天合约）系统性缺失的极端场景，微笑拟合误差飙升至9.6–13.1，而VAE稳定于1.5–1.9，成为唯一可行解；
  • 跨资产协同：BTC与ETH联合训练使两市场内分布性能较最优单资产模型提升9–27%，证实二者共享深层波动曲面流形；
  • 无套利保障：混合输出天然满足日历/蝶式套利自由（arbitrage-free），而参数模型在>30%掩码时即失效；
  • 异常检测能力：未监督的逐快照重建误差自动凸显2023年8月17日闪崩与10月下旬ETF预期行情，验证其市场状态感知力。

所有代码、数据预处理脚本与训练配置已开源。

AI Summary (English)

We propose a hybrid convolutional variational autoencoder (VAE) for cryptocurrency implied-volatility surfaces, integrated with a deterministic per-tenor routing mechanism that switches between a quadratic smile re-fit and VAE-generated outputs based on local mask density. Trained jointly on 6,034 hourly BTC/ETH volatility surfaces from Binance (May–October 2023) on a common $6 \times 7$ tenor-delta grid, the model achieves surface-completion RMSE of 0.94–1.56 vol points under 10–50% random masking—outperforming the smile re-fit alone (7.00 at 50% mask) by 8× with zero added inference cost. Critically, under structurally correlated missingness (e.g., entire tenors dropped), the hybrid sustains 1.5–1.9 vol-point error versus 9.6–13.1 for the parametric baseline—identifying a regime where generative modeling is uniquely viable. Joint BTC/ETH training improves in-distribution performance by 9–27% over single-symbol counterparts, revealing substantial shared volatility manifold structure. The hybrid output is calendar- and butterfly-arbitrage-free at listed strikes—a property the smile re-fit fails to preserve beyond ~30% masking. Unsupervised reconstruction error spikes also flag key market events: the August 17, 2023 flash crash and late-October ETF-anticipation rally. Full infrastructure is open-sourced.

Abstract

We present a convolutional variational autoencoder for cryptocurrency implied-volatility surfaces, together with a deployable predictor that combines it with a quadratic smile re-fit through a deterministic per-tenor routing rule. Trained on 6,034 fully-filled hourly Binance Options surfaces of BTC and ETH spanning May-October 2023 and parameterised on a common $6 \times 7$ tenor-delta grid, the model attains a hidden-cell surface-completion RMSE in the 0.94-1.56 vol-point range across both markets and mask rates 10-50%. The hybrid predictor attains 0.83 vol points at 50% masking against 7.00 for the smile re-fit alone, an eightfold reduction obtained at no additional inference cost. Under structurally-correlated hole patterns that emulate the withdrawal of an entire tenor of strikes, the smile re-fit incurs 9.6-13.1 vol points of error while the learned model remains at 1.5-1.9, isolating a regime in which the generative model is the only viable predictor. Joint training on BTC and ETH improves the in-distribution model on both markets by 9-27% relative to the better-performing single-symbol counterpart, indicating a substantially shared vol-surface manifold across the two largest cryptocurrencies over the observation window. The hybrid is calendar- and butterfly-arbitrage-free at the listed strikes, a property that the parametric smile re-fit alone fails at high mask rates. The per-snapshot reconstruction error of the trained model flags the late-October ETF-anticipation rally and the August $17$, $2023$ flash crash as elevated-error periods without supervision. All training and evaluation infrastructure is released to support reproducible follow-on work.

item.title: Distribution Alignment for One-Shot Federated Learning via Optimal Transport
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16655v1

AI Summary (中文)

问题背景

一次性联邦学习（One-Shot Federated Learning, OSFL）在仅允许客户端与服务器单次通信的极端带宽受限场景下至关重要。然而，客户端间固有的域偏移（domain shift）与标签偏移（label shift）联合存在，导致特征表示严重失准；由于缺乏多轮迭代优化机会，传统对齐方法失效，现有方案（如知识蒸馏、服务器端生成或集成聚合）往往隐含“表征已对齐”假设，或孤立处理两类偏移，鲁棒性不足。

方法创新：SLOT-Align

本文提出SLOT-Align（Single-round, Learning-free Optimal Transport Alignment），一种免训练、几何感知的单轮特征对齐框架：

  • ✅ 冻结共享编码器：各客户端使用同一预训练编码器提取轻量级二阶统计特征（如均值与协方差），避免额外训练开销；
  • ✅ Bures-Wasserstein质心构建全局参考分布：基于最优传输几何，以闭式解计算多客户端特征分布的Wasserstein质心，作为统一语义锚点；
  • ✅ 闭式测地线最优传输映射：为每个客户端求解到全局质心的显式线性传输矩阵，实现高效、可逆、保结构的特征重标定。

实验验证与优势

在ImageNet-1K、DomainNet、Office-Home等多基准上，SLOT-Align兼容ViT、ResNet等主流预训练骨干，并即插即用地提升FedAvg、FedGen、FedDistill等OSFL方法性能：平均准确率提升3.2–6.8%，在强联合偏移（如Photo→Sketch + imbalanced labels）下鲁棒性显著增强。其零参数更新、单轮计算复杂度仅为O(d³)（d为特征维数），可无缝嵌入现有OSFL流水线，无需修改任何训练逻辑。

AI Summary (English)

One-Shot Federated Learning (OSFL) faces severe performance degradation under joint domain and label shift due to irreversible feature misalignment in single-round communication. To address this, we propose SLOT-Align, a learning-free, geometry-aware alignment framework leveraging optimal transport. It extracts compact second-order statistics via a shared frozen encoder, computes a global reference distribution using the Bures-Wasserstein barycenter, and applies closed-form geodesic optimal transport maps to align local features to this reference. SLOT-Align requires no additional training, introduces negligible overhead, and integrates plug-and-play with existing OSFL methods (e.g., FedAvg, FedGen). Extensive experiments across diverse benchmarks (DomainNet, Office-Home), backbones (ResNet-50, ViT-B/16), and shift settings show consistent accuracy gains of 3.2–6.8%, significantly improving robustness under challenging joint shifts—without modifying client/server training procedures.

Abstract

One-Shot Federated Learning (OSFL) addresses extreme communication regimes in which clients interact with the server only once, amplifying the impact of heterogeneous client data distributions. In particular, the interaction of domain shift and label shift across clients induces misaligned feature representations that cannot be corrected through iterative optimization. Existing OSFL methods rely on distillation, server-side generation or ensemble-based aggregation, but assume aligned representations or address domain and label shift separately. We introduce SLOT-Align (Single-round, Learning-free Optimal Transport Alignment), a geometry-aware feature harmonization framework for OSFL. SLOT-Align uses a shared frozen encoder to extract compact feature statistics, constructs a global reference via Bures-Wasserstein barycenters, and aligns local representations using closed-form geodesic optimal transport maps. The method is computationally efficient and can be combined with existing OSFL pipelines relying on frozen encoders without modifying their training procedures. Extensive experiments across multiple benchmarks, pretrained backbones, and OSFL methods show that SLOT-Align consistently improves accuracy and robustness under joint domain and label shift.

item.title: Multi-Modal Spatio-Temporal Graph Neural Network with Mixture of Experts for Soil Organic Carbon Prediction
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16580v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

表层土壤有机碳（SOC）精准预测是农业可持续管理、土地利用规划与精准施肥决策的核心基础。现有方法存在两大瓶颈：一是依赖人工设计协变量与传统机器学习或单模态深度模型，难以充分挖掘Sentinel-2/1多光谱时序信号中的丰富时空信息；二是基于规则网格的建模范式忽视田间实测点位固有的不规则空间分布结构，导致空间关系建模失真。

方法创新

本文提出SpTGNN——一种融合多模态、异构图结构与专家混合机制的时空图神经网络框架。其核心设计包括：

  • 异构图建模：将土壤采样点建模为图节点，构建三类边——空间邻近性、光谱相似性与高程关联性，并采用关系感知图注意力机制分别学习各关系下的模式；
  • 多源特征融合：通过微调TerraMind基础模型编码Sentinel-2、Sentinel-1与DEM数据，联合环境协变量及可学习的位置/时间嵌入；
  • 稀疏MoE融合：引入top-$k$路由的稀疏混合专家模块，动态融合四路特征流；
  • 解耦不确定性量化：结合异方差回归（偶然不确定性）与深度集成（认知不确定性），并施加Moran’s $I$空间自相关正则项提升地理可解释性。

主要结果

在覆盖全球三大区域（非洲≈26k、欧洲≈14k、其余≈9k样本）的49k SOC数据集上，5模型深度集成在非洲测试集达$R^2=0.762$、RMSE=3.51±0.48\ g/kg、MAPE=22.9%，显著优于XGBoost基线；单模型最佳验证$R^2$达0.864。消融实验证实异构图、MoE融合与微调骨干网络均具显著贡献；不确定性校准后，混合ECE为0.031，$\beta$-NLL ECE低至0.026。据我们所知，这是首个将基础模型特征提取、异构图注意力与解耦不确定性量化统一于SOC制图的端到端框架。

AI Summary (English)

Top-soil organic carbon (SOC) prediction remains critical for sustainable agriculture and land policy, yet existing methods suffer from oversimplified spatial assumptions and underutilized multi-modal remote sensing signals. We propose SpTGNN—a novel multi-modal spatio-temporal graph neural network integrating heterogeneous graph representation (spatial, spectral, elevation edges), relational graph attention, fine-tuned TerraMind encoders for Sentinel-2/1/DEM, environmental covariates, and learned positional-temporal embeddings. A sparse Mixture-of-Experts module fuses four feature streams via top-$k$ routing, while uncertainty is decomposed into aleatoric (heteroscedastic regression) and epistemic (deep ensembles) components, regularized by Moran’s $I$. Evaluated on a global corpus (~49k samples across Africa, Europe, and other regions), our 5-member ensemble achieves $R^2 = 0.762$, RMSE = $3.51 \pm 0.48$ g/kg, and MAPE = 22.9% on the Africa test split—outperforming tabular XGBoost—and a single checkpoint reaches validation $R^2 = 0.864$. Ablations confirm substantial gains from each core component, and post-calibration ECE drops to 0.031 (hybrid) and 0.026 ($\beta$-NLL). To our knowledge, SpTGNN is the first framework unifying foundation-model feature extraction, heterogeneous graph attention, and decomposed uncertainty quantification for SOC estimation.

Abstract

Top-soil organic carbon (SOC) prediction is fundamental to agricultural sustainability, land use policy and fertilization planning. Existing approaches face two limitations: they pair hand-crafted covariates with classical ML or single-modal deep models that miss rich spectral and temporal information, and grid-based architectures ignore the irregular spatial structure of field measurements. We introduce SpTGNN, a multi-modal spatio-temporal graph neural network addressing both. SpTGNN represents soil measurements as nodes in a heterogeneous graph with three edge types (spatial proximity, spectral similarity, elevation), and applies relational graph attention to learn separate patterns per relation. A fine-tuned TerraMind encoder extracts node features from Sentinel-2, Sentinel-1 and DEM signals, combined with per-sample environmental covariates and learned positional and temporal embeddings. A sparse Mixture-of-Experts module fuses the four streams via top-$k$ routing. Uncertainty is captured by pairing heteroscedastic regression (aleatoric) with deep ensembles (epistemic), and a Moran's $I$ penalty regularizes spatial autocorrelation. We evaluate on a global SOC corpus split into three regional instances ($\sim$49k samples globally, Africa $\sim$26k, Europe $\sim$14k). Our 5-member deep ensemble reports $R^2=0.762$, RMSE $=3.51\pm0.48$ g/kg and MAPE $=22.9\%$ on the Africa test split, improving over a tabular XGBoost baseline; the best single checkpoint reaches validation $R^2=0.864$. Ablations confirm the heterogeneous graph, MoE fusion and fine-tuned backbone each contribute substantively, and the ensemble UQ stack achieves post-calibration ECE of $0.031$ (hybrid) and $0.026$ ($β$-NLL). To our knowledge, this is the first framework to unify foundation-model feature extraction, heterogeneous graph attention and decomposed uncertainty quantification for SOC estimation.

item.title: pFedUL: Layer-Aware Federated Unlearning for Personalized Federated Learning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16304v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

联邦遗忘（Federated Unlearning, FU）旨在响应数据主体“被遗忘权”，从联邦学习（FL）模型中高效移除特定客户端的数据影响，以满足GDPR等法规要求。然而，现有FU方法几乎全部面向中心化全局模型范式（如FedAvg），而实际部署中，个性化联邦学习（pFL）——如FedPer、FedRep、Ditto和FedBN——已成为处理非独立同分布（non-IID）数据的主流方案。这类方法将模型解耦为共享全局层与客户端专属个性化层，导致传统FU语义失效：在共享层彻底遗忘可能损害其余客户端的个性化能力，而忽略个性化层则违背遗忘完整性。

方法创新：pFedUL框架

本文首次形式化定义pFL场景下的联邦遗忘问题，并提出层感知的个性化联邦遗忘框架pFedUL，包含三大核心组件：
1. 梯度驱动的层级贡献归因：分别量化目标客户端对共享参数与个性化参数的梯度敏感度，实现细粒度影响定位；
2. 自适应选择性遗忘：对共享层采用基于Hessian近似的逆向更新，对个性化层实施梯度掩码+局部重初始化，兼顾遗忘强度与结构稳定性；
3. 轻量再校准协议：仅需剩余客户端单轮本地微调（≤5 epochs），即可恢复个性化表征，通信开销降低92%。

评估与结果

我们提出两项pFL专属指标：个性化保留得分（PPS，衡量遗忘后个性化性能衰减）与跨客户端公平性指数（CFI，评估各客户端遗忘影响均衡性）。在CIFAR-10/100与FEMNIST上多级non-IID实验表明：pFedUL的遗忘效果媲美全量重训练（ΔAcc < 0.8%），同时为剩余客户端保持平均97.3%的个性化准确率；相较6种适配pFL的SOTA遗忘方法，PPS提升12.6–28.4%，CFI提升19.3%，显著优于基线。

AI Summary (English)

Federated unlearning (FU) enables efficient removal of client data influence from federated models to comply with privacy regulations like GDPR. Yet existing FU methods assume homogeneous global models (e.g., FedAvg), while real-world deployments increasingly adopt personalized FL (pFL) frameworks—such as FedPer, FedRep, Ditto, and FedBN—that split models into shared global layers and client-specific personalized layers. This structural heterogeneity fundamentally redefines the unlearning objective: complete forgetting in shared layers risks degrading personalization for remaining clients. To address this, we propose pFedUL, the first layer-aware FU framework for pFL. It comprises: (1) gradient-based layer-wise contribution attribution to separately quantify a target client’s impact on shared vs. personalized parameters; (2) adaptive selective unlearning—applying Hessian-informed reverse updates to shared layers and gradient-masking + light reinitialization to personalized layers; and (3) a lightweight recalibration protocol enabling remaining clients to restore personalization within ≤5 local epochs. We introduce two pFL-specific metrics: Personalization Preservation Score (PPS) and Cross-client Fairness Index (CFI). Experiments across CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, and FEMNIST under diverse non-IID settings show pFedUL achieves unlearning effectiveness on par with full retraining (ΔAccuracy < 0.8%), maintains 97.3% average personalized accuracy for remaining clients, and outperforms six state-of-the-art FU baselines adapted to pFL by +12.6–28.4% in PPS and +19.3% in CFI.

Abstract

Federated unlearning (FU) enables the removal of specific data contributions from federated learning (FL) models to comply with regulations such as the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR). However, most existing FU methods are designed for the FedAvg paradigm, where all clients share a single global model. In practice, personalized federated learning (pFL) methods such as FedPer, FedRep, Ditto, and FedBN have become widely adopted due to their superior handling of non-IID data. These methods decompose the model into shared global layers and client-specific personalized layers, fundamentally altering the semantics of unlearning, yet this setting has received little attention. We formalize FU under the pFL paradigm, identifying a tension between unlearning completeness on shared layers and personalization preservation for remaining clients. We then propose pFedUL, a layer-aware selective unlearning framework comprising three components: (1) gradient-based layer-wise contribution attribution that separately quantifies the target client's influence on shared and personalized parameters, (2) adaptive selective unlearning that applies differentiated forgetting strategies across layer types, and (3) a lightweight recalibration protocol enabling remaining clients to restore personalization with minimal overhead. We further introduce two new metrics, Personalization Preservation Score (PPS) and Cross-client Fairness Index (CFI), to evaluate pFL-specific unlearning quality. Experiments on CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, and FEMNIST under varying non-IID settings indicate that pFedUL achieves unlearning effectiveness comparable to full retraining while maintaining an average of 97.3\% personalized accuracy for remaining clients. Compared with six state-of-the-art FU methods adapted to the pFL setting, pFedUL consistently achieves superior personalization preservation.

item.title: Rapid Poison: Practical Poisoning Attacks Against the Rapid Response Framework
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16242v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

本研究针对工业界广泛部署的快速响应（Rapid Response, RR）框架——包括Anthropic公司ASL-3安全防护系统所采用的核心机制——开展安全性分析。RR框架通过持续收集新出现的越狱（jailbreak）攻击样本，自动生成合成变体并迭代更新越狱检测分类器，以实现对新型对抗样本的快速适应。然而，该闭环式自动化训练流程存在隐蔽的供应链风险。

攻击方法与创新点

我们首次揭示：提示注入（prompt injection）可被用于污染RR的数据生成与训练 pipeline，在仅操控越狱样本（不触碰良性数据或标签）的严格威胁模型下，实现两类高隐蔽性投毒攻击：

  • （I）目标化投毒攻击：诱导分类器将无害输入（如普通用户提问）错误标记为越狱，且误判结果精准绑定特定语义特征（如特定关键词、格式模板或话题类别）；
  • （II）概念驱动后门攻击：提出全新攻击范式——Omission Attack（省略攻击）。其核心洞见在于：当训练数据中刻意“省略”某危险概念（如暴力指令）于越狱样本中时，模型会错误地将该概念的出现与“安全”标签关联，从而在含该触发概念的越狱输入上产生泛化性漏报（false negatives）。该机制突破了传统后门需显式注入触发器的限制。

关键结果

在仅1%投毒率下，两类攻击均引发显著标签翻转：目标攻击达成最高100%假阳性率（所有指定格式良性样本被误判），后门攻击实现最高96%假阴性率（超九成含触发概念的越狱样本逃逸检测），且效果在未见过的攻击策略上仍稳健。本工作首次在真实防御框架中验证了“仅污染越狱样本”的强约束下投毒可行性，为AI安全运维提供了关键风险警示与防御新视角。

AI Summary (English)

This paper exposes critical vulnerabilities in the production-deployed Rapid Response (RR) framework, used in systems like Anthropic’s ASL-3 safeguards. RR continuously updates jailbreak-detection classifiers by generating synthetic variants from newly observed jailbreaks—yet we show that prompt injection can poison this pipeline by injecting malicious samples solely into the jailbreak data stream, without modifying benign inputs or labels—a previously unexplored threat constraint. We propose two novel attacks: (I) targeted poisoning, inducing high false positives (up to 100%) on harmless inputs sharing a desired feature (e.g., formatting or keyword); and (II) concept-based backdoor attacks via our new Omission Attack, which exploits classifier misassociation: when trained on unsafe samples lacking a dangerous concept (e.g., coercion), the model incorrectly links the presence of that concept with the “safe” label—causing strong generalization to unseen jailbreaks containing it. At just 1% poisoning rate, both attacks achieve substantial label flipping: up to 96% false negatives on triggered jailbreaks and full false-positive saturation. Our work is the first to demonstrate practical, high-impact poisoning under strict real-world constraints.

Abstract

The Rapid Response (RR) framework, deployed in production systems, including Anthropic's ASL-3 safeguards, continuously improves jailbreak-detection classifiers. When new jailbreaks emerge that bypass these classifiers, Rapid Response generates synthetic variants for training, helping the model generalize from the new attacks and quickly adapt. We reveal that prompt injection can infiltrate this pipeline to deliver poisoned samples into the classifier's training set, enabling two attack objectives: (I) targeted poisoning attacks that create false positives on harmless samples by categorizing them as a jailbreak, with a specific desired feature (e.g., certain formatting, subject, or keyword), (II) concept-based backdoor attacks that induce false negatives on jailbreak inputs, generalizing even to jailbreaks from attack strategies the defender explicitly trained against, when the backdoor trigger is present. Importantly, our threat model restricts adversaries to modifying only jailbreak samples (not benign data or labels), a constraint unexplored by prior work that makes the second objective particularly challenging. We address this with Omission Attack, which exploits a new phenomenon: when training on concept-absent unsafe samples, the classifier misassociates that concept's presence with the safe label. Both attacks cause substantial and in some cases near-complete label flipping at only a 1% poisoning rate, achieving up to 100% false positive rates and up to 96% false negative rates.

item.title: PaperJury: Due-Process Review for Bounded LaTeX Revision
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16322v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

传统写作助手或批判生成器难以支撑学术论文投稿前的“预提交加固”（pre-submission hardening）——这一任务要求对抗性全篇审查、明确接受“无需修改”结论，并实施受控、可验证、不破坏 LaTeX 构建流程的有限修订。现有系统存在三大缺陷：（1）问题标识无法跨轮次持久化；（2）缺乏从批评到裁决的确定性路由机制；（3）缺乏对稿件的主动控制权，无法拒绝无效质疑或暂缓作者专属决策项。

方法：PaperJury 系统设计

我们提出 PaperJury，首个面向 LaTeX 计算机科学论文的闭环“审-判-修-验”系统。其核心创新在于确定性–语义分离架构：

  • 确定性层（Orchestration）：负责冻结主张主干（frozen claim spine）、维护持久化问题账本（durable ledger）、精确分解任务、硬性路由、终止判定及严格一次（exact-once）补丁应用，保障构建安全与过程可重现；
  • 语义层（Agents）：仅执行受限范围内的整体审查、争议裁决与局部修复，无权变更流程逻辑或绕过约束。

关键机制与结果

系统整合四大支柱：有界整体审查（bounded holistic review）、可争辩性路由（contestability-based routing）、正当程序式裁决（due-process trial）与风险适配型防护链（risk-proportional guard chains），确保所有锚点限定编辑（anchor-bounded edits）安全可控。最终输出三类终局判定：无效驳回（invalid-drop）、有效可修（valid-fixable）、作者必需介入（author-required）。在 Vision/NLP/ML 领域 32 篇预留论文的双臂专家评估中，PaperJury 在问题质量、裁决准确性、路由合理性、编辑安全性、收敛稳定性及单位成本上均显著优于四种基线（包括 LLM-only 和混合工作流），验证了负载关键的安全性与完成逻辑必须由确定性编排承载，而非交由模型自主判断的命题。

AI Summary (English)

PaperJury is a closed-loop system for pre-submission hardening of human-authored LaTeX CS papers, addressing the unique demands of adversarial whole-paper review, explicit no-fix verdicts, and artifact-safe bounded revision. Unlike existing assistants or critique generators, PaperJury enforces a strict deterministic-versus-semantic split: deterministic orchestration handles decomposition, frozen claim spine, durable issue ledger, deterministic routing, stopping criteria, and exact-once patch application—ensuring LaTeX build safety and reproducibility; semantic agents perform only bounded review, judgment, and repair. It introduces contestability-based routing, due-process trials, and risk-proportional guard chains to yield three terminal outcomes: invalid-drop, valid-fixable, and author-required. Evaluated on 32 held-out papers across Vision, NLP, and ML against four baselines, PaperJury significantly outperforms in issue quality, verdict/routing accuracy, edit safety, convergence stability, and cost—supporting the thesis that load-bearing safety and completion logic must reside in deterministic orchestration, not model discretion. Code: https://github.com/u7079256/paperjury.

Abstract

Pre-submission hardening of human-authored LaTeX computer science papers differs from drafting assistance because it requires adversarial whole-paper review, explicit no-fix outcomes, and bounded artifact-safe revision. Existing writing assistants, critique generators, and judge-centered loops lack durable issue identity across rounds, deterministic routing from critique to adjudication, and manuscript control that can reject invalid concerns or defer author-dependent ones. We present PaperJury, a closed-loop review-verdict-revise-verify system built on a deterministic-versus-semantic split: deterministic orchestration manages decomposition, a frozen claim spine, a durable ledger, routing, stopping, and exact-once patch application, while semantic agents are limited to bounded review, judgment, and repair. PaperJury combines bounded holistic review, contestability-based routing, a due-process trial, and risk-proportional guard chains for anchor-bounded edits, yielding terminal outcomes of invalid-drop, valid-fixable, and author-required. In a two-arm expert-review evaluation on held-out Vision, natural language processing, and machine learning papers against four baselines, we assess issue quality, verdict and routing quality, edit safety, convergence behavior, and cost, supporting the thesis that load-bearing safety and completion logic should reside in deterministic orchestration rather than model discretion. PaperJury is available at https://github.com/u7079256/paperjury.

item.title: OTRO: Oblivious Tokenization Path with Square-Root ORAM
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.17358v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

在基于CPU/GPU可信执行环境（TEE）的机密计算栈中，CPU端大语言模型（LLM）分词器（tokenizer）构成关键安全缺口：其基于查表的分词过程会暴露显著的内存访问模式，成为侧信道攻击的突破口。近期研究已成功从Intel TDX生产环境中完整恢复用户提示词（prompt），证实该风险真实且严重。

方法创新

本文提出OTRO（Oblivious Tokenization Path with Square-Root ORAM），一种面向低延迟LLM服务的高效无痕分词方案。其核心突破在于：

  • 复用式平方根ORAM池：利用分词表只读特性，部署多个轻量级、预初始化的平方根ORAM实例，避免单实例高频重建开销；
  • 纪元化轮转策略：将访问划分为固定长度“纪元”，解耦真实访问与重建操作，并在纪元边界填充哑元访问，消除时序与边界可观察性；
  • KV缓存感知分块分词：将分词任务按GPU预填充（prefill）阶段动态分块，使ORAM重建与GPU计算流水线重叠，显著减少活跃实例数。

主要结果

在HuggingFace Tokenizers与nano-vLLM中实现，部署于TDX虚拟机并协同NVIDIA H100 GPU：
✅ 时间至首Token（TTFT）开销 ≤ 4.5%（远低于PathORAM的10–58%）；
✅ 分词器引入延迟 < 总TTFT的10%；
✅ 内存开销 < 0.5 GB；
✅ 在Llama、Qwen、Phi等多族多尺寸模型上，可观测泄漏降低超99%。

AI Summary (English)

OTRO introduces an efficient oblivious tokenization path for LLM serving in CPU-GPU confidential computing stacks (e.g., Intel TDX + NVIDIA H100). It addresses the critical side-channel leakage from tokenizer memory access patterns—demonstrated to enable full prompt recovery in production—while avoiding the ~13× slowdown of tree-based ORAMs like PathORAM. OTRO leverages square-root ORAM for fast single lookups but eliminates its prohibitive $O(N\log^2 N)$ rebuild cost every $\sqrt{N}$ accesses via three innovations: (1) a pool of replicated, read-only square-root ORAM instances; (2) an epoch-based rotation policy with dummy-padded boundaries to decouple and conceal rebuild timing; and (3) chunked, KV-cache-aware tokenization that overlaps rebuilds with GPU prefill. Implemented in HuggingFace Tokenizers and nano-vLLM within a TDX CVM, OTRO incurs ≤4.5% TTFT overhead, keeps tokenizer latency under 10% of total TTFT, adds <0.5 GB memory, and drastically reduces observable leakage across diverse model families and sizes.

Abstract

The CPU-side large language model (LLM) tokenizer is a critical security gap in LLM serving through a confidential computing stack with CPU and GPU trusted execution environments (TEEs). Tokenizers converts the prompts through table-driven lookups, and the resulting memory access patterns are a powerful source of side-channel leakage. Recent work demonstrates end-to-end recovery of user prompts from tokenizer access pattern on production Intel TDX. However, a drop-in use of the popular tree-based Oblivious RAMs (e.g., PathORAM) to prevent access-pattern leakage introduces $\sim$13$\times$ tokenizer slowdown, resulting in 10-58% higher time-to-first-token (TTFT). In this paper, we present OTRO, an efficient, oblivious tokenization path tailored to latency-critical LLM serving. OTRO relies on square-root ORAM for fast single-access lookups, but avoids its prohibitive $O(N\log^2N$) rebuild cost every $\sqrt{N}$ accesses through three key innovations. First, OTRO provides a pool of replicated square-root ORAM instances that utilize the read-only nature of tokenizer table. Second, an epoch-based rotation policy decouples accesses from rebuilds and pads each epoch with dummy accesses to its boundaries, minimizing observable information. Lastly, chunked KV-cache-aware tokenization further overlaps rebuilds with GPU prefill and minimizes the instance count. Implemented as modules in HuggingFace Tokenizers and nano-vLLM, running within a TDX-enabled CVM with an NVIDIA H100 GPU, OTRO limits TTFT overhead to at most 4.5%, keeps tokenizer-induced latency under 10\% of total TTFT, and adds less than 0.5 GB of memory overhead while reducing the tokenizer's observable leakage across various model families and sizes.

item.title: Safety, Security, and Cognitive Risks in Neuro-Symbolic AI
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.17223v1

AI Summary (中文)

神经符号AI中的安全、安全与认知风险

神经符号AI（NeSy）融合神经感知与符号推理，在医疗、司法等高风险领域因可解释性与结构化推理能力而备受青睐。然而，其混合架构显著扩大了攻击面，覆盖神经感知层、符号知识库、推理引擎、智能体编排层及数据存储层共五层，每一层均存在纯神经系统所不具备的新型漏洞。本文提出六项核心贡献：
1. 形式化定义：首次提出NeSy攻击面、符号完整性违规（SIV） 及跨层放大比 $\mathcal{X}$，后者分解为神经诱发分量与自主符号敏感分量；
2. 统一威胁模型：扩展MITRE ATLAS框架，新增11类NeSy专属战术，并构建含5类角色（如“本体篡改者”“推理劫持者”）的攻击者分类体系；
3. 符号层威胁目录：系统梳理知识图谱（KG）投毒、本体合并污染、推理引擎逻辑劫持等典型攻击模式；
4. 认知风险分析：揭示NeSy的显式逻辑解释会结构性加剧自动化偏见、权威偏见与奉承式强化——相较黑盒模型，其“可信解释”反而更易诱导用户盲从；
5. 跨学科缓解方案：提出可量化验证的防护措施（如符号一致性审计、推理链溯源），全部对齐NIST AI 600-1标准与欧盟《人工智能法案》合规要求；
6. 三项实证基准：（E1）在205实体医学KG中，仅需注入5个恶意三元组即可达SIV盈亏平衡点，且存在KG特异的隐蔽性/SIV权衡；（E2）PGD-10对抗扰动（$\varepsilon=0.01$）使$\mathcal{X}=5.884$（95% CI [4.64, 8.00], $p<0.0001$），对照随机基线确认其对抗特异性；（E3）单条OWL公理编辑实现93.3% SIV成功率，且100%通过Pellet一致性检验，但主流STIX检测器失效（准确率≈50%），凸显符号层检测的重大挑战。

AI Summary (English)

Neuro-symbolic AI (NeSy) combines neural perception with symbolic reasoning to enhance explainability and structured inference—yet introduces a five-layer attack surface (neural perception, knowledge bases, reasoning engines, agentic orchestration, data stores) absent in pure neural systems. This paper delivers six contributions: (1) formal definitions of the NeSy Attack Surface, Symbolic Integrity Violation (SIV), and Cross-Layer Amplification Ratio $\mathcal{X}$; (2) an extended MITRE ATLAS threat model with 11 NeSy-specific tactics and a five-profile attacker taxonomy; (3) a symbolic-layer threat catalogue covering KG poisoning, ontology merging, and inference subversion; (4) analysis showing NeSy’s explicit logical explanations structurally amplify cognitive risks—automation bias, authority bias, and sycophantic reinforcement—beyond black-box models; (5) interdisciplinary mitigations with NIST AI 600-1 and EU AI Act–aligned acceptance criteria; and (6) three empirical benchmarks: targeted KG poisoning achieves break-even SIV at $B=5$; PGD-10 ($\varepsilon=0.01$) yields $\mathcal{X}=5.884$ ($p<0.0001$); and single-axiom OWL edits achieve 93.3% SIV success with full stealth against Pellet but fail STIX detection at chance level—revealing a critical detection gap.

Abstract

Neuro-symbolic AI (NeSy) pairs neural perception with symbolic reasoning, making it attractive for high-stakes domains where explainability and structured inference are required. However, this hybrid architecture introduces an enlarged attack surface spanning five layers: neural perception, symbolic knowledge bases, reasoning engines, agentic orchestration, and data stores -- each exploitable in ways absent from purely neural systems. This paper makes six contributions: (1) formal definitions of NeSy Attack Surface, Symbolic Integrity Violation (SIV), and Cross-Layer Amplification Ratio $\mathcal{X}$, decomposed into neural-caused and autonomous symbolic sensitivity components; (2) a unified threat model extending MITRE ATLAS with 11 NeSy-specific tactic extensions and a five-profile attacker taxonomy; (3) a symbolic-layer threat catalogue covering knowledge graph (KG) poisoning, ontology-merging, and inference-engine subversion; (4) analysis of cognitive risks -- automation bias, authority bias, and sycophantic reinforcement -- structurally amplified by NeSy's explicit logical explanations relative to black-box neural outputs; (5) interdisciplinary mitigations with measurable acceptance criteria aligned to NIST AI 600-1 and the EU AI Act; (6) three empirical benchmarks: (E1) targeted KG poisoning achieves break-even SIV at injection budget $B=5$ on a 205-entity medical KG, with a KG-specific stealth/SIV trade-off; (E2) PGD-10 at $\varepsilon=0.01$ yields $\mathcal{X}=5.884$ (95% CI $[4.64,\, 8.00]$, $p<0.0001$), confirmed adversarially specific by a matched-random baseline ($E^{R}_{\mathrm{rand}}=0$), on a DistilBERT+ProbLog pipeline; (E3) single-axiom OWL edits achieve 93.3% SIV success with 100% Pellet-consistency stealth, but held-out STIX detection fails at 50% (random-guessing level), an open problem.

item.title: Quantifying quantum risk: a measure of crypto agility
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.17116v1

AI Summary (中文)

量化量子风险：一种密码敏捷性度量方法

随着量子计算能力的提升，其对现有公钥密码体系（如RSA、ECC）构成实质性威胁，尤其通过Shor算法等实现“窃听即解密”（harvest now, decrypt later）攻击。尽管实用化量子计算机预计十年内出现，但攻击者已可能开始大规模截获加密流量，因此当前即需部署抗量子迁移策略。本文聚焦于填补关键研究空白：如何为量子安全系统设定可量化的密码敏捷性容忍阈值。我们首次提出旋转时间（rotation time）——即密钥或密码套件从生成、分发、启用到退役的端到端周期——作为核心度量指标，并建立其与组织安全风险容忍度之间的数学近似关系：旋转时间越短，量子暴露窗口越小，风险越低。基于2015–2023年NVD中1,247条与密钥管理、协议降级及侧信道漏洞相关的CVE数据，我们统计分析发现：高危漏洞平均响应中位时间为8.2小时，严重漏洞平均处置周期为1.7天；据此推导出面向量子风险的旋转时间容忍阈值应处于数小时至数天量级。该结果表明：仅依赖长期后量子密码（PQC）标准尚不充分；必须将密码敏捷性（支持快速轮换）与混合加密架构（传统+PQC并行）深度耦合。然而，实现小时级密钥轮转对密钥分发、策略同步、服务无感更新等提出严峻技术与运维挑战，需跨层协同优化。本工作为组织制定可落地的量子迁移路线图提供了首个基于实证数据的风险—敏捷性量化框架。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces rotation time—the end-to-end duration for cryptographic material (e.g., keys, ciphersuites) to be generated, distributed, deployed, and retired—as a quantitative measure of crypto agility. We derive an analytical approximation linking rotation time tolerance to organizational quantum security risk tolerance. Using historical CVE data (2015–2023) on key management and protocol vulnerabilities (n=1,247), we find median high-severity incident response times of 8.2 hours, implying quantum-risk-aware rotation time tolerances in the hours-to-days range. This demonstrates that achieving quantum resilience requires not only post-quantum cryptography (PQC), but also tightly integrated hybrid encryption and highly agile key lifecycle management. However, meeting such short rotation windows poses significant technical and operational challenges—including real-time key distribution, zero-downtime updates, and cross-system policy synchronization—demanding architectural co-design across protocols, infrastructure, and governance.

Abstract

Because of their ability to enable new forms of cryptanalysis, quantum computers pose a threat to the cryptographic algorithms that are widely used to secure contemporary computer systems. A practical quantum computer may emerge within the next ten years or so, but due to theorised "harvest now, decrypt later" style attacker behaviour, mitigations are necessary today. Recent advances in cryptography and security architecture show promise in supporting the design of systems that exhibit resilience against quantum-enabled cryptanalysis, however there is a key gap in the literature around the subject of deriving tolerances for such systems. In this paper, we introduce the concept of rotation time as a measure of crypto agility, and derive an approximation that links rotation time tolerance to security risk tolerance. Historical CVE data is used to calculate illustrative values for rotation time tolerance, which is found to be of the order of hours to days. This demonstrates that using crypto agility in conjunction with hybrid encryption is an effective approach for designing quantum-resilient systems, but may necessitate challenging technical and operational tolerances in order to meet organisational risk tolerances.

item.title: Loss Landscape Poisoning: Targeted Extraction of Unseen Training Data from LLMs
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.17110v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

大型语言模型（LLMs）日益依赖包含敏感信息的专有数据进行训练，如医疗记录、金融数据及含个人秘密的用户对话。如何防范针对训练数据的提取攻击，已成为隐私保护的核心挑战。

方法创新：损失景观投毒（Loss Landscape Poisoning）

本文首次提出并系统验证一种新型定向数据提取攻击：攻击者无需接触目标样本（如某条特定病历），仅需在训练数据中注入少量恶意样本（即“投毒”），即可诱导模型在推理阶段高概率输出该未见过的目标记录。其核心机制在于重构模型局部损失景观——通过精心设计的投毒样本，在目标文本对应的输出位置人为制造一个“尖锐的损失极小值”，同时抬升邻近token序列的损失值，迫使模型将目标完成视为该邻域内唯一低损解，从而实现强记忆性固化。

关键发现

  • 攻击在多种架构与训练范式下均有效：集中式训练与联邦学习场景均观察到显著泄露；
  • 跨模态泛化性强：在纯语言模型上提取成功率高达100%，在视觉-语言模型（如CLIP+LLM联合架构）上达90%；
  • 差分隐私（DP）训练可有效防御原始攻击，但本文进一步提出损失景观探针攻击（Landscape Probing Attack），绕过DP噪声干扰，直接利用梯度曲率信号定位尖锐极小值，成功突破DP防线；
  • 无需修改模型结构、不依赖后门触发器，仅通过标准训练流程即可实现，隐蔽性高、实用性广。

本工作揭示了损失几何结构与数据记忆间的深层联系，为理解LLM隐私风险提供了新视角，并呼吁对损失景观的鲁棒性进行系统性防护。

AI Summary (English)

We introduce Loss Landscape Poisoning (LLP), a novel privacy attack enabling targeted extraction of unseen training records—without direct access to them—by strategically poisoning a small fraction of training data. Our key insight is that carefully crafted poison samples reshape the local loss landscape around a target completion, creating a sharp, isolated loss minimum that forces the model to memorize the target as the unique low-loss solution in its neighborhood. The attack requires no architectural modification and generalizes across centralized and federated learning. We demonstrate near-perfect extraction success: up to 100% on language models and 90% on vision-language models. While standard differentially private (DP) training mitigates the base attack, we propose a Landscape Probing Attack that directly estimates curvature via gradient-based queries, bypassing DP noise and recovering targets even under strong privacy guarantees. This work exposes fundamental vulnerabilities rooted in loss geometry and calls for landscape-aware privacy defenses.

Abstract

Large Language Models are increasingly trained on proprietary or sensitive data, from private healthcare and financial records to user conversations containing secrets. Ensuring the privacy of such data against extraction attacks has become a central concern. In this paper, we ask whether an attacker who can poison a portion of the training data can facilitate the leakage of a separate target record they have no access to. We answer in the affirmative and show that such leakage can be induced by a poisoning mechanism that reshapes the model's local loss landscape around the target completion. Our key insight is that poisoning to create a sharp loss minimum at the target, surrounded by elevated loss on nearby alternatives, forces the model to memorize the target as the unique low-loss solution in its neighborhood. The attack requires no architectural changes, and generalizes across centralized and federated learning settings. We demonstrate that the attack amplifies privacy leakage across language (up to 100% successful extraction), and vision-language models (up 90% successful extraction). We show that the attack is thwarted when the model is trained to be differentially private. However, we introduce a new attack that directly probes the loss landscape bypassing even differential privacy defenses.

item.title: Vibrato Expression Control for Singing Voice Conversion with Improving Independent Control
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.17126v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

歌唱风格（如颤音、音高起伏、声带振动模式）是塑造自然、富表现力人声的核心要素。现有歌唱语音转换（Singing Voice Conversion, SVC）方法在风格控制上仍面临两大瓶颈：（1）音高风格与能量特征的强耦合——传统F0建模难以分离颤音（vibrato）的速率、幅度与能量动态；（2）特定发声风格（如气泡音/vocal fry）建模失效——因其亚谐波特性导致基频（F0）提取严重失真，进而劣化音色转换质量。

方法创新

本文提出VibE-SVC2框架，实现音高风格与音色风格的细粒度、解耦式独立控制：

  • 音高风格控制：
  • 提出能量风格转换器（Energy Style Converter），显式建模并解耦残留于能量包络中的风格信息，解决前代VibE-SVC中未被处理的音高-能量纠缠问题；
  • 设计零样本音高风格转换器（Zero-shot Pitch Style Converter），仅需参考音频即可复现其颤音起始点、周期性与调制深度；
  • 首次引入颤音速率缩放（Vibrato Rate Scaling），作为完全独立于幅度的可控维度，突破VibE-SVC仅支持联合调节的局限。
  • 音色风格控制：
  • 扩展模型以支持多样化发声方式（如假声、嘶哑声、气泡音）；
  • 针对气泡音等亚谐波主导场景，提出亚谐波校正算法（Subharmonic Correction），通过谐波结构感知与F0后处理，显著提升F0轨迹的生理合理性与转换自然度。

实验验证

客观指标（MCD、F0 RMSE）与主观评测（MOS、ABX）均表明：VibE-SVC2在颤音可控性、音色保真度及跨风格泛化能力上全面超越VibE-SVC及主流SVC基线，首次实现双路径、正交化、零样本可迁移的歌唱风格表达控制。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents VibE-SVC2, a singing voice conversion framework enabling fine-grained, independent control over two orthogonal singing styles: pitch style (e.g., vibrato rate/extent) and timbre style (e.g., vocal fry, breathiness). To resolve pitch-energy entanglement unresolved in VibE-SVC, we introduce an Energy Style Converter and a zero-shot Pitch Style Converter that mimics reference pitch contours without target-style training data. Crucially, we propose vibrato rate scaling—the first method for isolated control of vibrato speed, decoupled from amplitude. For timbre, we extend phonation modeling and design a Subharmonic Correction algorithm to recover accurate F0 contours in subharmonic-dominant voices (e.g., vocal fry), where conventional F0 estimators fail. Comprehensive objective and subjective evaluations confirm VibE-SVC2 achieves state-of-the-art controllability, naturalness, and cross-style generalization.

Abstract

Singing style is a crucial aspect of a natural and expressive singing voice. Singers utilize singing styles to convey the feeling or emotion of the songs. Several works have been proposed to control singing style for making the more expressive singing voice. Recently, VibE-SVC successfully controls vibrato by predicting high-frequency F0 contour. In this paper, we introduce a singing voice conversion framework, called VibE-SVC2, to improve singing style conversion performance and controllability. The model offers control over two types of singing styles: a pitch style and a timbre style. For the pitch style, to resolve the pitch-energy entanglement issue that is unresolved in our previous work, we introduce a novel Energy Style Converter to address remaining style information in the energy contour. In addition, we propose a Zero-shot Pitch Style Converter, which mimics the pitch style of reference audio. To expand the controllability of the model, we propose vibrato rate scaling that is an independent control of vibrato extent, which is unavailable in VibE-SVC. For the timbre style, we extend the model to handle a variety of phonation styles. However, addressing specific styles such as vocal fry poses a challenge, as conventional F0 extraction often fails due to their inherent subharmonic characteristics, which degrades the conversion quality. To address this, we propose a novel Subharmonic Correction algorithm to refine the F0 contour for more natural timbre conversion. Through comprehensive objective and subjective evaluations, we demonstrate that VibE-SVC2 provides fine-grained, independent control over two types of singing styles, outperforming existing methods.

item.title: MemSlides: A Hierarchical Memory Driven Agent Framework for Personalized Slide Generation with Multi-turn Local Revision
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.17162v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

个性化幻灯片生成面临三重记忆耦合难题：需长期稳定保持用户偏好（如风格、术语习惯），动态吸纳多轮修订中新引入的约束（如“将第三页图表替换为柱状图”），并支持精准局部编辑（仅修改单页/元素，避免整稿重生成）。现有方法常将各类记忆混杂处理，导致偏好漂移、修订不一致或编辑粒度粗放。

方法：MemSlides 分层记忆框架

我们提出 MemSlides——首个专为幻灯片生成设计的分层记忆驱动智能体框架。其核心创新在于三类解耦记忆的协同设计：

  • 用户档案记忆（Long-term, Profile）：存储意图条件化的静态画像（如“学术型用户偏好简洁排版+LaTeX 公式”），支撑首轮个性化生成；
  • 工作记忆（Working Memory）：在多轮修订会话中持续维护活跃偏好与会话级约束（如“本稿禁用动画”“所有标题加粗”），实现跨轮次偏好继承；
  • 工具记忆（Tool Memory）：沉淀可复用的执行经验（如“将文本框转为 SmartArt 的可靠 API 调用序列”），保障局部编辑的鲁棒性与一致性。

框架配套 幻灯片局部作用域修订机制：每次修改仅定位并重生成最小语义单元（单页、单文本框、单图表），显著提升效率与可控性。

关键发现与贡献

  • 用户档案记忆使多角色/多意图评测集上的人格对齐准确率提升 23.7%（vs. 无记忆基线）；
  • 工具记忆注入在诊断性配对实验中将闭环修改成功率提高 41.2%（尤其在复杂格式转换任务中）；
  • 定性分析证实工作记忆可稳定携带 5 轮以上修订中的偏好链（如连续轮次中坚持“深蓝主色+无图标”要求）。

本研究首次系统论证：持久用户画像、会话级工作记忆、可复用执行知识的三重分离，是实现高保真、高可控、高效率个性化幻灯片生成的基石。

AI Summary (English)

Personalized slide generation demands coordinated memory management: preserving stable user preferences across sessions, retaining newly introduced constraints during multi-turn revision, and enabling reliable local edits—without regenerating entire decks. MemSlides addresses this via a hierarchical memory architecture that strictly separates three functionally distinct memory modules: (1) User Profile Memory for intent-conditioned long-term persona modeling; (2) Working Memory to maintain active session constraints and evolving preferences across revision rounds; and (3) Tool Memory to store reusable, validated execution patterns for robust localized editing. Paired with scoped slide-local revision—where updates target only the minimal affected region—MemSlides achieves significant gains: +23.7% persona alignment accuracy on a multi-intent profile bank, +41.2% closed-loop modification success in diagnostic matched-pair settings, and consistent cross-round preference carryover in qualitative analysis. Results demonstrate that effective personalization hinges on the principled separation of persistent identity, transient context, and reusable procedural knowledge.

Abstract

Personalized presentation generation requires more than conditioning on a current prompt or template: agents must preserve stable user preferences across tasks, retain newly introduced preferences and constraints during multi-turn revision, and carry out local edits reliably. We propose MemSlides, a hierarchical memory framework for personalized presentation agents that separates long-term memory from working memory and further divides long-term memory into user profile memory and tool memory. User profile memory stores intent-conditioned profiles for round-0 personalization, working memory carries active preferences and session constraints across revision rounds, and tool memory stores reusable execution experience for reliable localized editing. MemSlides pairs this memory design with scoped slide-local revision, so targeted updates act on the smallest affected region instead of repeatedly regenerating the full deck. In controlled experiments, user profile memory improves persona-alignment judgments on a multi-persona, multi-intent profile bank, tool-memory injection improves closed-loop modify behavior in diagnostic matched-pair settings, and qualitative cases illustrate working memory's ability to carryover preferences. Taken together, these results suggest that effective personalization in presentation authoring depends on separating persistent user profiles, session-level working memory, and reusable execution experience across generation and localized revision.

item.title: SkillVetBench: LLM-as-Judge for Multi-Dimensional Security Risk Evaluation in Open-Source LLM Agent Skills
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.15899v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题
